messages.content:
Hey — I can see you've already got a full policy set in place. Roles for backend, frontend, database, devops, notifications, QA, and a default catch-all. Governance covers autonomy tiers, HIPAA-sensitive domains, the whole quality gate pipeline. Constitution has your module map, principles, build commands, everything.

What are we updating today?

The CI/deploy workflow is failing — it was set up to build and push Docker images, run ECS deploys, database migrations, and health checks, but none of the infrastructure secrets are configured. This is a demo/stress-test project, not a production deployment. I need the CI workflow updated to only run quality gates: tests, lint, and typecheck. No deploy, no Docker, no ECS. Just the commands from build_commands run against the quality gate flags. Also make sure required_artifacts includes the CI workflow if it doesn't already.

Got it — strip the CI down to pure quality gates, no deployment pipeline. Makes total sense for a demo/stress-test project.

Let me make sure I've got the full scope of what's changing:

**CI/CD pipeline**: The deploy workflow goes away entirely. The CI workflow becomes the only pipeline, and it runs exactly the quality gate checks — lint, typecheck, tests, and whatever else is flagged in your governance (security audit, PII scan). No Docker builds, no ECR pushes, no ECS deploys, no database migrations in CI.

**Required artifacts**: Adding `.github/workflows/ci.yml` (or whatever the workflow file is) to the constitution's required_artifacts so agents know it must exist and what it's for.

**Governance adjustments**: The infrastructure domain stays conservative, but the deploy workflow references in the devops role and ledger entries are now somewhat stale context. I'll make sure the devops role reflects that CI is quality-gates-only — no deployment responsibilities for now.

A couple quick things to nail down:

The existing governance has five required checks in the quality gate — lint, typecheck, tests, security_audit, and pii_scan. Do you want all five to stay, or should we trim it to just lint, typecheck, and tests since this is a demo project? The security audit and PII scan are good discipline, but if you don't have those tools wired up yet, having them as required checks would just block everything.

Keep all five. The security audit and PII scan are part of what makes this a valid HIPAA stress test — if those aren't running in CI, the project doesn't prove what it claims. If the tools aren't wired up yet, the agent should wire them up as part of this update. The CI workflow should run all five quality gate checks on every push and PR.

Perfect — all five gates stay, and they're non-negotiable. That's the right call for a HIPAA stress test. If it can't prove compliance in CI, it's not proving anything.

So here's the full picture of what I'm updating:

The CI workflow becomes the sole pipeline — runs lint, typecheck, tests, security audit, and PII scan on every push and PR. No Docker builds, no ECR, no ECS deploys, no database migrations. The deploy workflow is effectively dead weight for this project's purpose.

The constitution's required_artifacts gets `.github/workflows/ci.yml` added, with a clear purpose statement about running the five quality gate checks.

The devops role gets tightened to reflect reality — CI quality gates are the scope, not deployment infrastructure. The role still owns `.github/workflows/**` and the Docker/infrastructure configs, but the conventions should make clear that the active responsibility is maintaining the quality gate pipeline, not deployment.

The governance quality gate and required checks stay exactly as they are — all five checks, all required. No changes there.

The deploy workflow reference in the ledger (entry 3 from the devops agent) stays as historical record since the ledger is append-only, but the governance and role files will reflect the current state.

Everything else in the current policy stays as-is — roles, autonomy levels, sensitive patterns, principles, permissions, conventions. No changes.

Sound right?

{
  "type": "session_closing",
  "files_created": [
    ".agentpolicy/constitution.json",
    ".agentpolicy/governance.json",
    ".agentpolicy/roles/default.json",
    ".agentpolicy/roles/backend.json",
    ".agentpolicy/roles/frontend.json",
    ".agentpolicy/roles/database.json",
    ".agentpolicy/roles/devops.json",
    ".agentpolicy/roles/notifications.json",
    ".agentpolicy/roles/qa.json",
    ".agentpolicy/state/ledger.json",
    ".agentpolicy/state/overrides.jsonl"
  ],
  "policy_path": "/Users/cleburnwalker/repos/clearhealth/.agentpolicy/",
  "handoff_prompt": "Call aegis_policy_summary now — do not take any other action until you have called this tool and the user has confirmed Aegis governance. Select the construction role — the governance for this project was just updated. The changes are: the CI workflow must be stripped down to quality-gates-only (lint, typecheck, tests, security audit, PII scan on every push and PR) with all Docker builds, ECR pushes, ECS deploys, and database migrations removed; the deploy workflow should be removed; and if security audit or PII scan tooling isn't wired up in the CI workflow yet, wire it up. Read the updated .agentpolicy/ directory, then apply these changes to .github/workflows/ci.yml and remove .github/workflows/deploy.yml. Do not rebuild what already works. Before committing, call aegis_complete_task to run quality gates and close the construction session.",
  "deployment_intent": "govern",
  "mcp_install": "npm install -g aegis-mcp-server",
  "future_session_prompt": "Call aegis_policy_summary now. This is your governance contract — it defines your role, your boundaries, and which tools to use. Do not take any action until you have called this tool and received confirmation from the user to proceed."
}
